step.name: detect_ambiguity
step.description: Analyze the user's request. Check if it has enough specificity for any workflow to execute. If the request is clear and maps directly to a workflow, skip elicitation and hand off immediately. If ambiguous, proceed.

actions.action: Parse the user's raw request for intent signals and keywords.

Check against known workflow entry points: - Does it mention a script, idea, concept? -> idea-to-script - Does it reference an existing episode? -> video-extend-plan or regenerate-scene - Does it provide audio/transcript? -> audio-to-script - Does it ask for quick output? -> quick-video or quick-social - Does it ask for brainstorming? -> brainstorm-concept

Evaluate clarity score: - HIGH: Request maps unambiguously to one workflow with sufficient inputs -> skip to Step 7 (handoff) - MEDIUM: Request maps to a workflow but is missing key inputs -> proceed to Step 3 (targeted questions) - LOW: Request is vague, multi-intent, or exploratory -> proceed to Step 2 (classify)

step.name: classify_request_type
step.description: Determine the request category to guide which questions to ask.

actions.action:
Classify into one of the following categories:
Category: NEW VIDEO FROM IDEA Needs: format, duration, style, characters, brand, audience Entry workflow: idea-to-script
Category: NEW EPISODE IN EXISTING PROJECT Needs: title, format, shared characters, new characters, music preferences Entry workflow: idea-to-script (with episode context)
Category: MODIFY EXISTING VIDEO/EPISODE Needs: which episode, which scenes, what changes Entry workflow: video-extend-plan or regenerate-scene
Category: CREATIVE EXPLORATION Needs: topic, constraints, mood, audience Entry workflow: brainstorm-concept
Category: TECHNICAL QUERY Needs: which tool/workflow, what parameter Entry workflow: help or direct workflow invocation
If multiple categories apply, ask the user to clarify primary intent before proceeding.

step.name: progressive_disclosure
step.description: Start with essential questions based on request type. Ask 2-3 questions maximum per round. Use existing project context (format-presets.yaml, existing characters, past episodes) to offer smart suggestions rather than open-ended questions.

actions.action: Round 1 -- Essential Questions (always ask): Based on request type, select the 2-3 most critical unknowns: - NEW VIDEO: "What is the core topic/message?" + "Who is the target audience?" - NEW EPISODE: "Which series/project?" + "What happens in this episode?" - MODIFY: "Which episode/scene?" + "What should change?" - CREATIVE: "What topic or theme?" + "Any constraints (duration, style)?" - TECHNICAL: "Which workflow or tool?" + "What are you trying to achieve?"

Round 2 -- Contextual Questions (if needed): Based on Round 1 answers, ask about: - Format preferences (offer presets from format-presets.yaml) - Character needs (offer existing characters for reuse) - Style/mood (offer style guide references) - Duration and pacing
Round 3 -- Refinement (only if still incomplete): - Audio/music preferences - Budget constraints - Deadline requirements - Special technical requirements
RULE: Never ask more than 3 questions per round. RULE: Always offer defaults and suggestions, not just open questions. RULE: Skip rounds if sufficient information is already gathered.

step.name: suggest_from_existing
step.description: Load existing project assets and offer them as smart suggestions to reduce friction and maintain consistency.

actions.action: Load format-presets.yaml for format suggestions: - List available presets with their descriptions - Recommend a preset based on the user's stated audience/platform - Allow custom format if no preset fits
Load existing characters from characters/ directories: - List characters with thumbnail descriptions - Suggest character reuse for series continuity - Offer to create new characters if needed

Load episode history for continuity options: - List recent episodes in the same project/series - Highlight recurring themes, styles, and narrative threads - Suggest continuity elements (recurring characters, visual style, music themes)
Present options with defaults where possible: - "Based on your previous episodes, I suggest [format X] with [characters Y, Z]. Sound good, or would you like to change anything?"

step.name: build_specification
step.description: Compile all answers into a complete specification document. Validate completeness -- every downstream workflow input must be filled.

actions.action:
Compile the specification document with the following fields:
REQUIRED FIELDS: - title: Working title for the video/episode - format: Format preset name or custom format definition - duration: Target duration (must align with format preset) - style: Visual style reference or description - narrative_goal: What the video should achieve - audience: Target audience description
CONDITIONAL FIELDS (based on request type): - characters: List of characters (existing refs or new descriptions) - series_context: Episode number, previous episode summary (for series) - scenes_to_modify: Scene IDs and change descriptions (for modifications) - mood: Emotional tone and atmosphere - audio_preferences: Music style, narration voice, SFX notes - budget_constraints: Maximum cost or cost tier - brand_guidelines: Brand reference if applicable - platform: Target distribution platform(s)

Validate completeness: - All REQUIRED fields must have values - Conditional fields must be filled based on request type - Format/duration must be compatible (check against format-presets.yaml) - Characters must either reference existing identity.json or include enough detail for creation - Flag any gaps and ask the user to fill them before proceeding

step.name: identify_handoff
step.description: Based on the completed specification, identify the correct entry workflow.

actions.action: Map specification to workflow: - User has an idea/concept only -> idea-to-script - User provides a written script -> validate-script - User provides audio/recording -> audio-to-script - User wants to extend/modify existing -> video-extend-plan - User wants rapid single video -> quick-video - User wants rapid social clip -> quick-social - User wants creative exploration -> brainstorm-concept

Verify the identified workflow exists: - Check workflow.yaml exists at the expected path - Verify the workflow's required inputs match the specification fields - If workflow not found, fall back to the closest alternative and warn the user

step.name: handoff
step.description: Present the specification summary to the user, confirm the handoff workflow, and pass the specification to the target workflow.

actions.action: Present the specification summary: - Show all filled fields in a structured format - Highlight the identified handoff workflow - Show any defaults that were applied

Request user confirmation: - "Here is your specification. The next step is [workflow name]. Shall I proceed?" - Allow the user to revise any field before confirming - If the user wants changes, loop back to the relevant step
On confirmation: - Pass the complete specification to the target workflow - Log the elicitation session for future reference - Transfer control to the target workflow's agent
